meta.calibration_note: Persona pricing bands are back-tested against one realized contract trace from a *completed* channel-partner engagement with a major SG Tier-2 consultancy subsidiary. The engagement ran a full annual cycle and is not currently in flight; the data point functions as historical operating proof and a price floor, not as forward revenue. Channel-partner tier is structurally distinct from direct-to-developer Tier-3 firm economics. Specific rate band and counterparty identity held internally.

personas.name: Tier-2 Consultancy Subsidiary (channel-services target)
personas.icp: Site-supervision specialist subsidiary of a major SG Tier-2 consultancy. Holds the QP(S) sign-off and public-sector EPPU panel access; periodically outsources S Pass RTO supply at body-shop economics under a payroll-services structure with statutory pass-through. Elitez has a *prior* completed annual engagement with one such counterparty (realized as a back-tested benchmark, not a live customer). Re-engagement and parallel agreements with peer Tier-2 subsidiaries (RSP, Meinhardt, KTP, Beca) are the addressable space for this persona.
pains: Pure body shops (Tier-4) cannot satisfy the increasing audit / ISO / data-protection requirements imposed by Tier-2 clients on their own suppliers
Permanent S Pass headcount has internal opportunity-cost; outsourcing the employment relationship frees the channel partner to deploy capital into licensed-firm capability instead
Talent recruitment overhead — Elitez handles JAC accreditation, MOM levy admin, repat, group insurance
personas.current_workaround: Typically 1-year framework agreements (renewable to 2–3 years). Top-tier-firm-drafted Service Agreement; monthly billing cycle. Loss-of-Employee convertibility fee is low, which preserves the channel partner's optionality to absorb deployed supervisors at end of term.

nba.summary: Realized channel-partner benchmark from one completed annual engagement: per-supervisor monthly billing reflects S Pass base salary + statutory pass-through (levy + WIC + group insurance) + payroll-services fee. The payroll-services fee is the Elitez gross margin. Sets a hard FLOOR for direct-to-developer S Pass billing post-2028 licence — Elitez should not undercut its own historical channel rate.

personas.name: Developer Tech Lead Dennis
personas.icp: Technical head at a mid-sized SG developer (CapitaLand-tier or below). Owns site supervision procurement across a multi-project pipeline. Reports to a Group Director who answers to the board on programme delivery and PQM compliance.
pains: Site supervision capacity is binary — either he locks in a partner now or scrambles 6 weeks before mobilisation
BCA 2028 firm-licensing creates a procurement screening criterion he cannot ignore for projects above S$75M
Body-shop suppliers can't satisfy the 12-month permanence rule, so pre-2028 shortlists are already shrinking
personas.current_workaround: Mix of in-house RTOs + 2–3 long-standing Tier-3 supplier relationships (typically RCY for the bigger packages, plus 1–2 mid-tier firms for swing capacity). PI insurance handled at developer level; documentation pain on every audit.

nba.summary: Anchored at RCY's Tier-3 rate card (~S$9,500/mo for senior RTO). Will accept a 5–10% discount for new-entrant relationship if Elitez can demonstrate retention infrastructure and licence-track credibility.

personas.name: Main Contractor PM Pete
personas.icp: Project manager at a BCA-listed main contractor (Kimly / Soilbuild / Woh Hup / Lum Chang). Bridges between developer technical heads and his on-site team. Procures supplementary RE/RTO supply when in-house team needs swing capacity.
pains: Replacement-on-demand SLA matters more than headline rate — a missing supervisor for 5 days = NCR cascade and milestone slip
JAC accreditation paperwork churn each time a supervisor moves between projects
Foreign-supervisor quota mechanics are a black box — he just needs the firm to hand him a deployable headcount
personas.current_workaround: 1–2 body-shop suppliers on framework agreement; manual call-off per project. Margins eroded when body shop can't fill within 2 weeks and Pete has to take an above-card senior-RE rate.

nba.summary: Pays body-shop rates + bears swing-capacity risk + JAC paperwork burden. Would consolidate onto Elitez Supply tier if SLA defensible — values the Elitez Group HR back-office over headline-rate discount.

personas.name: Consultancy Ops Olivia
personas.icp: Operations director at a Tier 1–3 consultancy (WSP, Beca, RSP, Surbana Jurong post-non-compete). Manages site supervision capacity for multiple ongoing engagements. Squeezed between fixed engineering staff costs and project-by-project supervision demand.
pains: Hiring/firing cycle on supervisors is too slow for project peaks — needs elasticity layer
Body shops compete on her cost base but can't satisfy her clients' BCA licence-readiness expectations from 2027 onward
Pre-existing supplier framework requires audit-grade docs she can't easily get from Tier-4
personas.current_workaround: In-house team for baseline + ad-hoc body-shop call-offs for peaks. Overhead on supplier audit per engagement.

nba.summary: Treats Elitez Supply as the elasticity layer — willing to pay parity with self-cost (~S$9,300 fully loaded for a senior RTO equivalent) for the back-office relief plus regulatory documentation transfer.

personas.name: Specialist Subcontractor Steven
personas.icp: Owner-operator of a façade or PPVC specialist subcontractor on a signature project (Marina Bay Sands expansion, Changi T5, Tuas Mega Port). Needs specialist supervision competency that mainstream Tier-3 firms don't carry.
pains: Mainstream Tier-3 firms supply general C&S supervisors who lack PPVC / post-tensioning / structural-steel specifics
Project owners expect specialist accreditation that body shops don't hold
Signature-project rate card supports premium pricing — but only if specialist competency is genuinely demonstrated
personas.current_workaround: Mix of in-house specialists (expensive, scarce) + ad-hoc Tier-1 consultancy supervision (premium-priced, slow to mobilise).

nba.summary: Pays Tier-1 rates today (~S$13,000 Senior RTO; up to S$16,500 Senior RE). Specialist tier gives Elitez 20–35% premium head-room above standard Tier-3 rates from Y2 onward via Adept Academy specialist tracks.

personas.name: Public Procurer Priya
personas.icp: Senior procurement officer at a public agency (LTA, HDB, JTC, BCA, PUB, CAG). Tenders QP(S) supervision packages explicitly. Constrained by tender language, PQM scoring, and post-2028 BCA-licence requirements.
pains: Pre-2028: needs to evaluate firms on licence-readiness, not just price — but tender frameworks haven't caught up
Post-2028: limited to BCA-licensed firms only; the shortlist contracts dramatically
PQM environmental sustainability now mandatory >S$50M — most Tier-3 firms can't score
personas.current_workaround: EPPU Grade-based screening + PSPC panel framework + multi-firm consortium awards (e.g., LTA Cross Island Line Ph1 Pkg2 across 5 firms).

nba.summary: Public-sector rates anchored above private (5–10%); rate is not the lever — licence + ESG + PQM scoring is. Elitez accesses public tenders via consortium with RCY-class partners pre-2028, then independently from M30.

pricing_models.name: Per-headcount monthly retainer (T&A)
pricing_models.rationale: Industry standard for Supply tier. Aligns to billable supervisor days.

pricing_models.name: Project-priced fixed fee (Manage tier)
pricing_models.rationale: Single point of accountability for the developer; absorbs capacity-management risk into Elitez.

pricing_models.name: Firm-licensed Certify retainer (post-2028)
pricing_models.rationale: BCA-licensed firm relationship for projects >S$75M with full ISO 17020 wrap.

pricing_models.name: Specialist day-rate premium (PPVC/MET/façade)
pricing_models.rationale: 20–35% premium over standard rates on signature projects; backed by Adept Academy specialist track.

pricing_models.name: Train tier — per-cohort SSG-funded course
pricing_models.rationale: B2B training revenue from external construction firms; SSG pays 50–90% of course fees.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Prior completed framework agreement with a Tier-2 SG consultancy subsidiary (annual cycle, expired Q4 2024) — top-tier-firm-drafted contract; standard 1-year term, sometimes renewable to 2–3 years. Pricing power lies on Elitez side: the channel partner gains licensed-firm-capacity-without-headcount value that exceeds the payroll-services margin Elitez extracts. NWC-aligned annual increment cap binds.
RCY-anchored mental ceiling; 5–10% discount for licence-track credibility wins relationship.
Body-shop comparison sets a floor at S$8,200 senior RTO; SLA reliability matters more than rate above floor.
Pays parity to internal cost for elasticity. Margin in time-saved-on-audit, not headline rate.
Signature-project rate card supports 20–35% premium. Competency matters; price is secondary.
Public-sector tender framework constrains. Licence + ESG + PQM scoring drives the award, not headline rate.

recommended_tiers.name: Channel Supply (re-activatable)
what_in: Time-and-attendance S Pass RTO supply to Tier-2 consultancy subsidiaries
Payroll services: monthly disbursement, MOM levy, statutory contributions
what_excluded: QP(S) sign-off — retained by client (this is not Tier-3 firm engagement)
Engineering opinion / structural sign-off — retained by client
PI insurance for engineering liability — not within scope
Direct billing to end-developer — channel-routed only
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Anchored on rate from a prior completed channel-partner Service Agreement (annual cycle, expired Q4 2024). Pricing floor based on the payroll-services fee on top of S Pass cost build (base salary + MOM levy + Work Injury Compensation + group insurance + SDL). Sets the hard floor for direct-to-developer S Pass billing post-2028 licence; also the rate to re-engage prior counterparty or sign similar agreements with peer Tier-2 subsidiaries.

recommended_tiers.name: Supply
what_in: Time-and-attendance supply of accredited RE/RTO (per supervisor per month)
PI insurance scaling S$5M Y1 → S$25M Y2 → S$50M Y3
what_excluded: Site Supervision Plan authorship (upgrade to Manage)
Specialist competencies pre-Y2 (upgrade to Specialist day-rate)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Lower end of the Tier-3 rate band (Tier-3 floor S$8,500–9,500). 10–15% discount to RCY in early-relationship period; step up to S$9,500 from Month 7.

recommended_tiers.name: Manage
what_in: Full site supervision team led by deployed RE
Repeat-assignment matching (same supervisor on developer's next project)
what_excluded: Full BCA-licensed firm engagement (upgrade to Certify post-2028)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Project-priced fixed fee per S$10–75M project. Anchored against developer's blended in-house cost + RCY-class outsourcing benchmark.

recommended_tiers.name: Certify
what_in: BCA-licensed Site Supervision Firm services (post Q1–Q2 2028)
Specialist coverage: PPVC, MET, advanced steel, façade
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: 7–10% upward repositioning post-licence. Target premium ~S$18,500 senior RE / Lead-equivalent from M24.

recommended_tiers.name: Train
what_in: WSQ-funded supervisor training via Adept Academy
JAC-recognised CPD courses (subject to JAC course approval)
Specialist track upskilling (PPVC, post-tensioning, structural steel)
SCTP / CCP funded reskill — up to 90% salary support
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Course-level pricing per supervisor (annual entitlement S$1,500 industry norm). 50–90% SSG funding cuts effective price; CCP cuts further for mid-career converts.

recommended_tiers.name: Specialist day-rate
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: 20–35% premium over standard Tier-3 rates. Anchored against Tier-1 senior RTO S$11,800 / Senior RE S$16,500 — captures the Tier-1 walk-away segment.

grants.name: WSQ (SSG)
grants.eligibility: WSQ-aligned courses delivered through SSG-approved Training Organisation (Adept Academy).

grants.name: SCTP — SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme
grants.eligibility: Mid-career converts into site supervision; salary support during training.

grants.name: CCP — Career Conversion Programme
grants.eligibility: Reskill into RTO competency for non-civil/structural diploma holders.

grants.name: PWCS — Progressive Wage Credit Scheme
grants.eligibility: Wage co-funding for lower-wage workers (raised to 30% in 2026, extended to 2028). Applicable to admin/support staff.
